title: Multi-tab assistant interface MVP (Claude Codex Copilot)
desc: Design a VS Code extension style multi-tab assistant interface that supports Claude, Codex, and Copilot tabs with a unified chat experience, while limiting scope to interface and orchestration behavior comparable to existing extension workflows.
steps: Define product boundary first: replicate interface behavior and orchestration only; do not attempt private backend cloning.
Use a three-pane layout: provider tabs on top, conversation thread center, composer and tool status footer.
Support provider tabs with isolated session state per provider and per workspace so users can switch tabs without context bleed.
Implement a shared message model with provider-specific adapters; keep one canonical event schema for user message, assistant delta, tool call, tool result, and final response.
Implement streaming UX parity: incremental token rendering, cancel button, retry last turn, and regenerate response per provider tab.
Provide workspace-aware context injection: active folder, selected files, and recent command history, with explicit user visibility and opt-out toggles.
Implement tool orchestration layer with permission gates for shell and file actions, plus non-blocking progress events in the UI timeline.
Provide robust failure UX: rate limit notices, provider timeout handling, reconnect retry policy, and clear fallback prompts instead of silent failures.
Implement cost and limit guardrails: estimated token usage per turn, session budget indicator, and configurable hard stop thresholds.
Track sessions in local structured storage keyed by workspace and provider; include compact summary snapshots for fast resume.
Define extension APIs as stable internal interfaces so UI and provider adapters can evolve independently without breaking chat state.
Deliver in phases: phase 1 UI skeleton and local mock streaming, phase 2 real provider adapters, phase 3 tool permissions and session resume, phase 4 polish and telemetry review.
author: claude
